top_categories.workout_context: 98ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At Tour of Georgia Gran Fondo, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Tour of Georgia Gran Fondo's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before April.
At mile 60 of 102, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
10,000ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Tour of Georgia Gran Fondo. Train it until it feels boring.
Tour of Georgia Gran Fondo's mountain passes forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 102 miles.
10,000ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Low-cadence force work for 10,000ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 102 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 10,000ft of climbing on mountain passes does to your power file on race day.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Tour of Georgia Gran Fondo, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Tour of Georgia Gran Fondo’s 102 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Tour of Georgia Gran Fondo. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Tour of Georgia Gran Fondo requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mountain passes to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on TT Threshold, Over Under, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 102 miles of mountain passes in Helen, Georgia.
